series.title: [PATCH] hw/display/vga-isa: Fix migration of the isa-vga device

review.summary: The patch correctly fixes isa-vga migration by introducing an ISAVGAState wrapper VMStateDescription and wiring dc->vmsd to it, so migration state is serialized into the embedded VGACommonState rather than the ISADevice base object. I did not find a functional regression in the code change. Dropping global_vmstate is a migration format change, but the justification is credible because migration for this device was already broken before this patch.

stages.A: Problem statement matches the code: using vmstate_vga_common directly on an isa-vga DeviceClass is invalid because the device object base is ISAVGAState/ISADevice, not VGACommonState.
stages.B: Implementation approach is consistent with existing QEMU patterns for wrapped VGA state: the new vmstate_vga_isa delegates migration to the embedded state field via VMSTATE_STRUCT.
stages.C: Behavioral impact is correct for the intended fix: new-to-new migration should target ISAVGAState.state and avoid the previous crash/corruption path.
stages.D: Compatibility impact exists because the section layout changes and global_vmstate is removed, but that is acceptable given the prior migration path was already non-functional for isa-vga.
stages.E: No additional code issues were identified from the patch itself beyond the non-functional checkpatch line-length warning.

disclaimer: LLM-generated draft. Not an authoritative review.
